text.title: django-testing Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to bring a Django test suite to the testing rules: pytest-django, factory data, real-code service tests, APIClient for DRF, transactional isolation.

list.item: Action: create `config/settings/test.py` (overrides DB to a test database, sets `EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django.core.mail.backends.locmem.EmailBackend'`). Pin `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE` to it in `[tool.pytest.ini_options]`.
Output: test runs use the test settings deterministically.
Input: inline data creation in tests.
Action: extract factories to `tests/factories.py` (factory_boy) or adopt `model_bakery.baker.make` for terse cases.
Output: factories or bakery calls only.
Input: service-layer tests that mock helpers / utils / models.
Action: invoke the real service. Use in-memory DB. Mock only HTTP, S3, third-party SDK, email backend.
Output: service tests catch real bugs; mock surface shrinks to boundary.
Action: replace with `rest_framework.test.APIClient`. Use `force_authenticate(user=user)` instead of login round-trips.
Output: tests run faster; auth and content negotiation correct.
Input: tests marked `@pytest.mark.django_db(transaction=True)` for no reason.
Action: switch to the default `@pytest.mark.django_db`. Keep `transaction=True` only when the test must commit (signals, raw SQL connection assumptions).
Output: suite returns to fast transactional isolation.
